homepage.title: Jo White, MP for Bassetlaw
homepage.meta_description: I'm Jo White, your local Member of Parliament for Bassetlaw. You can contact me or sign up to receive my regular email newsletters on my website.
homepage.text_excerpt: Jo White, MP for Bassetlaw Skip to content Home Contact Home Contact Jo White MP BASSETLAW'S STRONG VOICE KEEP UP TO DATE Subscribe to receive my regular email newsletter and online surveys. Your views are important to me. By completing the form, you agree to receive regular updates from Jo White MP. The information you provide will be processed in accordance with my Privacy Policy . Name Email Postcode Subscribe It is an honour and a privilege to have been elected to serve the people of Bassetlaw as your Member of Parliament. I want to be an MP who listens and understands the issues that matter to you. Please do sign up to receive my regular email newsletter and surveys. If you need my assistance, you can contact me using the form below. GET IN TOUCH As your local MP, I am here to help. You can contact me using the form below or you can email me: [email protected] Message First Name Surname Home Address Email Telephone Send 01909 212171 [email protected] LATEST NEWS FROM JO 18 Feb 2026 Potholes are the main concern for Bassetlaw residents Read 15 Oct 2025 Giving Children in Bassetlaw the Best Start in Life Read 14 Oct 2025 Digital ID will give people more control over their data Read 6 Oct 2025 Buying or Selling a Home will be Faster, Cheaper, and Easier Read 16 Sep 2025 Taking pride in our community is a form of patriotism, but vandalism isn’t Read 2 Sep 2025 Illegal immigration tops local concerns in Summer Survey Read Previous Page 1 Page 2 Page 3 Page 4 Next CAMPAIGNS SOCIAL MEDIA Comments Box SVG icons Used for the like, share, comment, and reaction icons Jo White MP 2 days ago It was great to hear the concerns of local people at another one of my coffee mornings in Worksop today. ☕ Thank you to everyone who came to speak with me about their concerns. ... See More See Less Jo White MP 3 days ago Big changes are coming to Council Tax collection in England as the Government are making it fairer and less aggressive for people who fall behind on payments 👇 ↔️ Spreading council tax payments over 12 months, making council tax more affordable ❌ Stopping sudden ‘full bill’ demands if a council tax payment is missed. ... See More See Less Jo White MP 3 days ago Update from the "Save DSA Campaign" on Doncaster Airport ... See More See Less Jo White MP 4 days ago ❓As part of my 'Ask me Anything' series, Craig asked me "With your colleague Ed Miliband's big push for a net-zero UK, why are electricity prices still connected to gas prices?" ⚡While this is currently true, the Government has a plan to de-couple the link between the price of gas and electric, bringing down energy bills for households and businesses right across Bassetlaw. ... pages.title: Potholes are the main concern for Bassetlaw residents - Jo White MP
pages.h1: Potholes are the main concern for Bassetlaw residents
pages.text_excerpt: Potholes are the main concern for Bassetlaw residents - Jo White MP Skip to content Home Contact Home Contact Potholes are the main concern for Bassetlaw residents Jo White MP 18 February 2026 I am getting a huge number of messages from people about potholes across Bassetlaw. Our roads are falling apart and it cannot go on like this. Many drivers tell me they are worried about damage to their cars. They say they have to swerve to avoid deep holes in the road. People are facing huge bills for car repairs to fix damage to tyres. I have even heard about license plates being ripped from cars by deep potholes in some areas. Recently, I was contacted by residents who live on Sheffield Road in Blyth. The road there is in a terrible state. Large lorries hit the crater holes at speed, and the impact is so strong that nearby houses shake, day and night. Some residents are struggling to sleep through the night because the vibrations are so bad. Some have even seen cracks appear in their brickwork. That is simply not good enough. I am pushing for the whole road to be properly resurfaced, not just patched up. I am pleased that this request has now been put forward to the County Council as part of next year’s roads budget. I will keep pressing for it to be approved. I see the ‘dob jobs’ taking place, but this is a quick fix and not a permanent solution. In some cases, the roads crumble again within weeks. The County Council recently spent £75,000 on flags for lampposts. While I love to see our flag flying, is this really the priority when our roads are in such a bad state? We have heard plenty of excuses. The councillor in charge of roads has even tried to blame my husband, John Mann, the former MP for Bassetlaw. But I have lived here in Bassetlaw for over 25 years, and I have never known our roads to be in such poor condition. Over the past few months, I have reported over 50 potholes across Bassetlaw. But when I go to report them, I often see that they have been reported many times before, and no action has been taken. Many people ask me what is being done about it. Here are the facts. Nottinghamshire County Council is in charge of looking after our roads. It runs this service through its company, VIA. The Council has been given an extra £8.3 million from the government to fix roads. This is on top of the £70 million it already has for road repairs. I plan to meet with the County Council very soon, alongside other Nottinghamshire MPs of all parties to discuss the issue. The Council is saying it plans to buy a special machine called the JCB Pothole Pro. It costs about £200,000 and is meant to repair potholes more quickly. However, I am concerned that the previous Conservative administration looked at this in 2021, and found that the machine did not save money, could damage kerbs, and had problems on narrow roads. Most importantly, it did not fix potholes any faster than normal repair methods. I will be keeping a close eye on how this money is spent. What matters most is that our roads are maintained and properly repaired. My message is clear, use the money and get on with the job. Contact [email protected] 01909 212171 / 01777 322128 The Crossing, Newcastle Street, Worksop, S80 2AT Jo White MP, New Street, Retford, DN22 6EG Social Facebook Youtube Linkedin About Privacy Policy Promoted by Daniel Henderson on behalf of Jo White, both at Labour Hall, New Street, Retford, DN22 6EG
